  "findings": [
    "Slings are rigged over sharp, unprotected edges, creating an immediate risk of sling failure and dropped loads."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop all lifting operations immediately.",
    "Lower any suspended loads to a safe resting position.",
    "Install engineered edge protection, softeners, or wear pads to protect the slings from sharp corners before resuming work."
  ],
  "rationale": "Rigging slings must be protected from sharp edges, corners, or protrusions to prevent cutting, tearing, or catastrophic failure during a lift. Operating without edge protection violates basic safety standards and manufacturer specifications.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251(c)(9)"
  ]
